title: Roadmap: release v1.3.0 — ship two weeks of agent-ergonomics + wiki work to the binaries that actually run
context: Everything since v1.2.3 is built, tested, and pushed — but the hook-side improvements are INERT on every machine until released: the post-commit hook runs the global binary, proven three times this session (stale hook minted a duplicate draft the new gate would have skipped; injection dedup and SessionStart orientation can't fire from source). Built ≠ deployed.
decision: Cut v1.3.0 per rb_cut_release_01: version bump, changelog + site entries for — drift-gated wiki extras (relation-hash links, prose-heal), specs ledger + doc adoption hardening, hunch now + the NOW page + human-vouched roadmap, retrieval priors + topic-chain promotion (Recall@10 90→100%, MRR 0.693→0.789, committed golden gate), hunch_now/hunch_wiki_status MCP, session-deduped hook injections + SessionStart orientation, content-matched blocking constraints + conformance dogfood, context task-fallback + moment-grouped grounding, draft-dedup coverage gate. Then npm i -g on both machines so the hooks go live — and the dedup gate's first real fire is observable.
